headline: Exclusive-BESI attracts takeover interest as advanced chip packaging demand surges, sources say
summary: BE Semiconductor Industries has been fielding takeover interest, as demand for its chip-packaging technology has become more ‌critical for semiconductor equipment makers, according to three people familiar with the matter.  The Amsterdam-listed ‌chip equipment maker, which has a market value of 14 billion euros ($16.20 billion), has been working with investment bank Morgan ​Stanley to evaluate the approaches, two of the people said, requesting anonymity as the discussions are confidential.  U.S. chip-equipment maker Lam Research is among the suitors that have held discussions with the Dutch company, one of the people said.

U.S. chip-equipment maker Lam Research is among the suitors that have held discussions with the Dutch company, one of the people said.

headline: Goldman Sachs Raises Price Targets on 3 Tech Giants by 10% and More
summary: Founded in 1869, Goldman Sachs is the world’s second-largest investment bank by revenue and ranks 55th on the Fortune 500 list of the largest U.S. corporations by total revenue. The Wall Street white-glove giant offers financing, advisory services, risk distribution, and hedging for the firm’s institutional and corporate clients. headline: 3 Nasdaq 100 Stocks to Research Further
summary: The Nasdaq 100 (^NDX) is packed with high-growth companies, and while the market is competitive, some are pulling ahead. A handful of standout businesses are continuing to scale, delivering strong financials and market leadership.

headline: Cramer: Buy Western Digital, Micron, Seagate on oil-driven dips
summary: Jim Cramer has a shopping list ready. He just needs oil to hand him the sale. “Western Digital, Seagate, SanDisk, and Micron could all be bought on a big move down because of oil. If we see $120 oil, those are the four stocks you’ve got to reach for.” That’s the thesis: these aren’t buys ... Cramer: Buy Western Digital, Micron, Seagate on oil-driven dips

headline: Shares of semiconductor companies are trading lower amid overall market weakness due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The industry may be pulling back due to high energy costs that may disrupt the chipmaking process.

headline: Defense Lifts Moat Stocks As Tech Stumbles
summary: Moat Index gained 2.13% in February, outperforming the S&P 500 by nearly 3 percentage points as defensive rotation favored its equal-weighted, valuation-conscious approach.
